code_changes.content:
# Javadoc Generation: Kafka Record Batch Serialization Classes

## Summary

Javadoc comments were added to three classes in
`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/`:

- `RecordBatch.java`
- `DefaultRecordBatch.java`
- `MemoryRecords.java`

No implementation logic was changed. All additions are documentation-only.

---

## `RecordBatch` (interface)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The sparse original comment was expanded to cover:

- **Purpose and protocol role** — explains that a `RecordBatch` is the fundamental unit of data
  transfer in the Kafka wire protocol, describes the three magic versions (v0/v1/v2) and the
  features each introduces (per-record timestamps, idempotent/transactional producers).
- **Usage context** — notes that instances are obtained from `Records.batches()` and provide both
  shallow batch-level metadata access and deep per-record access.
- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**; external synchronisation required
  for concurrent access. Notes that in practice batches are short-lived single-thread objects.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `MemoryRecords`, `DefaultRecordBatch`, and `Record`.

### Method Javadoc added or enhanced

| Method | Changes |
|--------|---------|
| `ensureValid()` | Added `@throws CorruptRecordException` tag. |
| `hasProducerId()` | Added `@return` tag clarifying the condition. |
| `streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)` | Added **Performance note** section explaining lazy decompression and the benefit of pooling `BufferSupplier` buffers. |
| `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()` | Expanded with numbered notes for the two edge cases (tie-breaking, v0 always empty) and a **Performance note** warning about the full-scan cost. |

---

## `DefaultRecordBatch` (class)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The existing comment (which already contained the wire-format schema and compaction semantics)
was retained verbatim and enhanced with:

- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**. Calls out that in-place mutation
  methods (`setLastOffset`, `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch`) and concurrent reads
  require external synchronisation.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `RecordBatch`, `MemoryRecords`, `MemoryRecordsBuilder`.

### Method Javadoc added

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `baseTimestamp()` | Documents the dual role of the base timestamp field: normally the timestamp of the first record; overloaded to store the delete horizon when that flag is set. References `deleteHorizonMs()`. |
| `recordInputStream(BufferSupplier)` | Documents the returned stream, the transparent decompression when compression is enabled, the performance note about closing the stream to release pooled buffers. |
| `writeEmptyHeader(...)` | Documents all 12 parameters, the purpose (preserve producer sequence on fully-compacted batches), the exact buffer overhead written, and the CRC computation. Cross-references `writeHeader`. |
| `writeHeader(...)` | Documents all 17 parameters, the purpose (low-level batch header serialisation used by `MemoryRecordsBuilder`), the CRC computation, and both `@throws IllegalArgumentException` conditions. |
| `sizeInBytes(long, Iterable<Record>)` | Documents parameters and the performance note (full record iteration, no compression overhead). |
| `sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord>)` | Same pattern as above; notes sequential offset-delta assumption. |
| `incrementSequence(int, int)` | Documents the Kafka modulo-`Integer.MAX_VALUE+1` sequence-number arithmetic and the wrap-around behaviour. Cross-references `decrementSequence`. |
| `decrementSequence(int, int)` | Inverse of `incrementSequence`; same arithmetic description. |

---

## `MemoryRecords` (class)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The original one-sentence comment was expanded to cover:

- **Purpose** — read or in-place-modify an existing encoded buffer; directs new-buffer producers to
  `MemoryRecordsBuilder` or the static factory methods.
- **Performance characteristics** — a bullet list covering O(1) batch-header access, lazy
  decompression for record-level access, lazy/cached `validBytes()`, and zero-copy semantics of
  `buffer()` and `slice()`.
- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**. Notes the shared-buffer risk of
  in-place mutations via `MutableRecordBatch` methods.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `MemoryRecordsBuilder`, `RecordBatch`, `DefaultRecordBatch`.

### Method Javadoc added or enhanced

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel)` | Added **Performance note** about the write loop and mark/reset buffer usage. |
| `validBytes()` | Added **Performance note** about lazy computation and O(1) cached result. |
| `filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)` | Expanded with two paragraphs: explains the two-level filter (batch → record), the re-serialisation into v2 batches, and the overflow-buffer behaviour. |
| `buffer()` | Documents zero-copy duplicate semantics: independent position/limit but shared bytes; in-place mutation visibility. |
| `slice(int, int)` | Documents zero-copy sub-range view, silent clamping to available bytes, and all three `@throws IllegalArgumentException` conditions. |
| `batches()` | Documents the re-iterable behaviour, the lazy iterator, and the performance note that batch metadata is read without decompression. |
| `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` | Documents no-copy semantics. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Documents all parameters. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long, int)` | Documents `maxSize` parameter; notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)` | Documents `logAppendTime` parameter. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, int)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch`. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, long, long, short, int, boolean)` | Documents transactional/idempotent overload (current magic, CREATE_TIME). |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int)` | Full producer-metadata overload without transactional flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, int)` | All fields except control-batch flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, int)` | Full overload including `isControlBatch`. |
| `withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents convenience defaults. |
| `withRecords(Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic-version overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `initialOffset` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic + offset overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents offset + leader-epoch overload. |
| `withIdempotentRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents idempotent producer identity fields and default values. |
| `withTransactionalRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents transactional producer fields and default values. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents no-producer-metadata overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, long, short, int, int, boolean, SimpleRecord...)` | Full overload — documents all 10 parameters, the size-estimation strategy, single-batch guarantee, and `EMPTY` return for empty input. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents default offset=0 and current-time timestamp. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents explicit timestamp overload. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, int, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents full offset + epoch overload. |
| `writeEndTransactionalMarker(...)` | Documents as a lower-level buffer-writing alternative. |
| `withLeaderChangeMessage(...)` | Documents KRaft control-batch semantics (no compression, CREATE_TIME). |
| `withSnapshotHeaderRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withSnapshotFooterRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withKRaftVersionRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withVotersRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |

---

## Quality checklist

| Requirement | Status |
|-------------|--------|
| Thread-safety stated for every class | ✅ All three classes explicitly state "not thread-safe" |
| Performance implications noted for serialization/deserialization | ✅ `streamingIterator`, `validBytes`, `filterTo`, `sizeInBytes`, `writeFullyTo`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp` |
| No Javadoc on private methods | ✅ Only public methods documented |
| No documentation of internal/private fields | ✅ No field Javadoc added |
| `@param`, `@return`, `@throws` tags used | ✅ Throughout |
| `{@link}` cross-references used | ✅ Throughout |
| No implementation logic changed | ✅ Pure documentation additions |

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Task: Generate Javadoc for Kafka Record Batch Serialization

**Repository:** github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b (mirror of apache/kafka)
**Output:** Write your summary to `/workspace/documentation.md`; edit source files directly

## Objective

Generate Javadoc comments for Kafka's record batch serialization classes in `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/`. These classes handle the binary encoding of Kafka message batches and are critical for correctness and performance.

## Scope

Document the following classes:
- `RecordBatch` — interface defining a batch of records
- `DefaultRecordBatch` — concrete implementation of a magic v2 batch
- `MemoryRecords` — in-memory collection of record batches

For each class, document:
- Class-level Javadoc: purpose, role in the Kafka protocol, thread-safety guarantee
- Key public methods: `@param`, `@return`, `@throws` tags
- Performance characteristics where relevant (e.g., lazy decoding, copy-on-write)
- Cross-references using `{@link}` to related classes

## Quality Bar

- Thread-safety must be explicitly stated for every class (thread-safe, not thread-safe, or conditionally thread-safe)
- Performance implications must be noted for serialization/deserialization methods
- Do not add Javadoc to private methods
- Use standard Javadoc tags: `@param`, `@return`, `@throws`, `@see`, `{@link}`

## Anti-Requirements

- Do not change implementation logic
- Do not document internal/private fields
Let me read the three target files from the repository.
The files are in the `internal/` subdirectory. Let me read all three now.
Now I have all three files. Let me create the local directory structure and write the documented versions.
Now let me write the three documented files:
Now let me write the summary documentation file:

The three files have been written with comprehensive Javadoc. Here is a summary of what was done:

---

## What was documented

### `RecordBatch.java` — interface

- **Class Javadoc** expanded from 4 lines to a full description covering: the three magic version epochs (v0/v1/v2) and their semantics, how instances are obtained via `Records.batches()`, explicit **not thread-safe** statement, and `@see` cross-references.
- **Method enhancements:** `ensureValid()` gained `@throws CorruptRecordException`; `hasProducerId()` gained a proper `@return`; `streamingIterator()` gained a **Performance note** on lazy decompression and buffer-pool reuse; `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()` gained numbered edge-case notes and a **Performance note** on full-scan cost.

### `DefaultRecordBatch.java` — concrete implementation

- **Class Javadoc** kept the existing wire-format schema and compaction semantics, and added an explicit **not thread-safe** statement calling out the in-place mutation methods.
- **New method Javadocs** for all previously undocumented public methods: `baseTimestamp()`, `recordInputStream()`, `writeEmptyHeader()` (12 params), `writeHeader()` (17 params, `@throws`), `sizeInBytes(long, Iterable<Record>)`, `sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord>)`, `incrementSequence()`, `decrementSequence()`.

### `MemoryRecords.java` — in-memory records collection

- **Class Javadoc** expanded to cover purpose, a bullet-list of **performance characteristics** (O(1) header access, lazy decompression, cached `validBytes()`, zero-copy `buffer()`/`slice()`), explicit **not thread-safe** statement, and `@see` cross-references.
- **New method Javadocs** for all public methods that lacked them: all `builder()` overloads (9 variants), all `withRecords()` / `withIdempotentRecords()` / `withTransactionalRecords()` variants, all `withEndTransactionMarker()` variants, `writeEndTransactionalMarker()`, all five KRaft control-record builders, `readableRecords()`, `buffer()`, `slice()`, and `batches()`.

{"content":"1: /*
2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with
4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0

10:  *

17: package org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;
18: 
19: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;
20: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;
21: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;
22: 
23: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;
24: import java.util.Iterator;
25: import java.util.Optional;
26: import java.util.OptionalLong;
27: 
28: /**
29:  * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),
30:  * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain
31:  * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records
32:  * regardless of compression.
33:  */
34: public interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {
35: 
36:     /**
37:      * The "magic" values
38:      */
39:     byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;
40:     byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;
41:     byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;
42: 
43:     /**
44:      * The current "magic" value
45:      */
46:     byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;
47: 
48:     /**
49:      * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp
50:      */
51:     long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;
52: 
53:     /**
54:      * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when
55:      * up-converting from an older format.
56:      */
57:     long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;
58:     short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;
59:     int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;
60: 
61:     /**
62:      * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is
63:      * first created by the producer.
64:      */
65:     int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;
66: 
67:     /**
68:      * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.
69:      *
70:      * @return true If so, false otherwise
71:      */
72:     boolean isValid();
73: 
74:     /**
75:      * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.
76:      */
77:     void ensureValid();
78: 
79:     /**
80:      * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.
81:      *
82:      * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long
83:      */
84:     long checksum();
85: 
86:     /**
87:      * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.
88:      *
89:      * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and
90:      * the value is updated during compaction.
91:      *
92:      * @return The max timestamp
93:      */
94:     long maxTimestamp();
95: 
96:     /**
97:      * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}
98:      * if the batch has magic 0.
99:      *
100:      * @return The timestamp type
101:      */
102:     TimestampType timestampType();
103: 
104:     /**
105:      * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will
106:      * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will
107:      * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return
108:      * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the
109:      * behavior is equivalent.
110:      *
111:      * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with
112:      * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.
113:      *
114:      * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record
115:      *         as described above).
116:      */
117:     long baseOffset();
118: 
119:     /**
120:      * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset
121:      * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log
122:      * compaction.
123:      *
124:      * @return The offset of the last record in this batch
125:      */
126:     long lastOffset();
127: 
128:     /**
129:      * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).
130:      *
131:      * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch
132:      */
133:     long nextOffset();
134: 
135:     /**
136:      * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).
137:      *
138:      * @return the magic byte
139:      */
140:     byte magic();
141: 
142:     /**
143:      * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.
144:      *
145:      * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none
146:      */
147:     long producerId();
148: 
149:     /**
150:      * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.
151:      *
152:      * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none
153:      */
154:     short producerEpoch();
155: 
156:     /**
157:      * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.
158:      */
159:     boolean hasProducerId();
160: 
161:     /**
162:      * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not
163:      * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.
164:      *
165:      * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none
166:      */
167:     int baseSequence();
168: 
169:     /**
170:      * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number
171:      * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log
172:      * compaction.
173:      *
174:      * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none
175:      */
176:     int lastSequence();
177: 
178:     /**
179:      * Get the compression type of this record batch.
180:      *
181:      * @return The compression type
182:      */
183:     CompressionType compressionType();
184: 
185:     /**
186:      * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.
187:      * @return The size in bytes of this batch
188:      */
189:     int sizeInBytes();
190: 
191:     /**
192:      * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case
193:      * for magic 2 and higher).
194:      *
195:      * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.
196:      */
197:     Integer countOrNull();
198: 
199:     /**
200:      * Check whether this record batch is compressed.
201:      * @return true if so, false otherwise
202:      */
203:     boolean isCompressed();
204: 
205:     /**
206:      * Write this record batch into a buffer.
207:      * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to
208:      */
209:     void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);
210: 
211:     /**
212:      * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.
213:      * @return true if it is, false otherwise
214:      */
215:     boolean isTransactional();
216: 
217:     /**
218:      * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon
219:      * @return timestamp of the delete horizon
220:      */
221:     OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();
222: 
223:     /**
224:      * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.
225:      * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown
226:      */
227:     int partitionLeaderEpoch();
228: 
229:     /**
230:      * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records
231:      * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming
232:      * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.
233:      *
234:      * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.
235:      *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)
236:      *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the
237:      *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant
238:      *                                    performance impact.
239:      * @return The closeable iterator
240:      */
241:     CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);
242: 
243:     /**
244:      * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).
245:      * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.
246:      *
247:      * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records
248:      */
249:     boolean isControlBatch();
250: 
251:     /**
252:      * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.
253:      * noted:
254:      * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp
255:      * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}
256:      * @return offset of max timestamp
257:      */
258:     default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {
259:         if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();
260:         long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();
261:         try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {
262:             while (iter.hasNext()) {
263:                 Record record = iter.next();
264:                 if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());
265:             }
266:         }
267:         return Optional.empty();
268:     }
269: }

arguments.content:
# Javadoc Generation: Kafka Record Batch Serialization Classes

## Summary

Javadoc comments were added to three classes in
`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/`:

- `RecordBatch.java`
- `DefaultRecordBatch.java`
- `MemoryRecords.java`

No implementation logic was changed. All additions are documentation-only.

---

## `RecordBatch` (interface)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The sparse original comment was expanded to cover:

- **Purpose and protocol role** — explains that a `RecordBatch` is the fundamental unit of data
  transfer in the Kafka wire protocol, describes the three magic versions (v0/v1/v2) and the
  features each introduces (per-record timestamps, idempotent/transactional producers).
- **Usage context** — notes that instances are obtained from `Records.batches()` and provide both
  shallow batch-level metadata access and deep per-record access.
- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**; external synchronisation required
  for concurrent access. Notes that in practice batches are short-lived single-thread objects.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `MemoryRecords`, `DefaultRecordBatch`, and `Record`.

### Method Javadoc added or enhanced

| Method | Changes |
|--------|---------|
| `ensureValid()` | Added `@throws CorruptRecordException` tag. |
| `hasProducerId()` | Added `@return` tag clarifying the condition. |
| `streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)` | Added **Performance note** section explaining lazy decompression and the benefit of pooling `BufferSupplier` buffers. |
| `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()` | Expanded with numbered notes for the two edge cases (tie-breaking, v0 always empty) and a **Performance note** warning about the full-scan cost. |

---

## `DefaultRecordBatch` (class)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The existing comment (which already contained the wire-format schema and compaction semantics)
was retained verbatim and enhanced with:

- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**. Calls out that in-place mutation
  methods (`setLastOffset`, `setMaxTimestamp`, `setPartitionLeaderEpoch`) and concurrent reads
  require external synchronisation.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `RecordBatch`, `MemoryRecords`, `MemoryRecordsBuilder`.

### Method Javadoc added

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `baseTimestamp()` | Documents the dual role of the base timestamp field: normally the timestamp of the first record; overloaded to store the delete horizon when that flag is set. References `deleteHorizonMs()`. |
| `recordInputStream(BufferSupplier)` | Documents the returned stream, the transparent decompression when compression is enabled, the performance note about closing the stream to release pooled buffers. |
| `writeEmptyHeader(...)` | Documents all 12 parameters, the purpose (preserve producer sequence on fully-compacted batches), the exact buffer overhead written, and the CRC computation. Cross-references `writeHeader`. |
| `writeHeader(...)` | Documents all 17 parameters, the purpose (low-level batch header serialisation used by `MemoryRecordsBuilder`), the CRC computation, and both `@throws IllegalArgumentException` conditions. |
| `sizeInBytes(long, Iterable<Record>)` | Documents parameters and the performance note (full record iteration, no compression overhead). |
| `sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord>)` | Same pattern as above; notes sequential offset-delta assumption. |
| `incrementSequence(int, int)` | Documents the Kafka modulo-`Integer.MAX_VALUE+1` sequence-number arithmetic and the wrap-around behaviour. Cross-references `decrementSequence`. |
| `decrementSequence(int, int)` | Inverse of `incrementSequence`; same arithmetic description. |

---

## `MemoryRecords` (class)

**File:** `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java`

### Class-level Javadoc added

The original one-sentence comment was expanded to cover:

- **Purpose** — read or in-place-modify an existing encoded buffer; directs new-buffer producers to
  `MemoryRecordsBuilder` or the static factory methods.
- **Performance characteristics** — a bullet list covering O(1) batch-header access, lazy
  decompression for record-level access, lazy/cached `validBytes()`, and zero-copy semantics of
  `buffer()` and `slice()`.
- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as **not thread-safe**. Notes the shared-buffer risk of
  in-place mutations via `MutableRecordBatch` methods.
- **`@see`** cross-references to `MemoryRecordsBuilder`, `RecordBatch`, `DefaultRecordBatch`.

### Method Javadoc added or enhanced

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel)` | Added **Performance note** about the write loop and mark/reset buffer usage. |
| `validBytes()` | Added **Performance note** about lazy computation and O(1) cached result. |
| `filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)` | Expanded with two paragraphs: explains the two-level filter (batch → record), the re-serialisation into v2 batches, and the overflow-buffer behaviour. |
| `buffer()` | Documents zero-copy duplicate semantics: independent position/limit but shared bytes; in-place mutation visibility. |
| `slice(int, int)` | Documents zero-copy sub-range view, silent clamping to available bytes, and all three `@throws IllegalArgumentException` conditions. |
| `batches()` | Documents the re-iterable behaviour, the lazy iterator, and the performance note that batch metadata is read without decompression. |
| `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` | Documents no-copy semantics. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Documents all parameters. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long, int)` | Documents `maxSize` parameter; notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)` | Documents `logAppendTime` parameter. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, int)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch`. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, long, long, short, int, boolean)` | Documents transactional/idempotent overload (current magic, CREATE_TIME). |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int)` | Full producer-metadata overload without transactional flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, int)` | All fields except control-batch flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, int)` | Full overload including `isControlBatch`. |
| `withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents convenience defaults. |
| `withRecords(Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic-version overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `initialOffset` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic + offset overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents offset + leader-epoch overload. |
| `withIdempotentRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents idempotent producer identity fields and default values. |
| `withTransactionalRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents transactional producer fields and default values. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents no-producer-metadata overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, long, short, int, int, boolean, SimpleRecord...)` | Full overload — documents all 10 parameters, the size-estimation strategy, single-batch guarantee, and `EMPTY` return for empty input. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents default offset=0 and current-time timestamp. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents explicit timestamp overload. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, int, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents full offset + epoch overload. |
| `writeEndTransactionalMarker(...)` | Documents as a lower-level buffer-writing alternative. |
| `withLeaderChangeMessage(...)` | Documents KRaft control-batch semantics (no compression, CREATE_TIME). |
| `withSnapshotHeaderRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withSnapshotFooterRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withKRaftVersionRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withVotersRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |

---

## Quality checklist

| Requirement | Status |
|-------------|--------|
| Thread-safety stated for every class | ✅ All three classes explicitly state "not thread-safe" |
| Performance implications noted for serialization/deserialization | ✅ `streamingIterator`, `validBytes`, `filterTo`, `sizeInBytes`, `writeFullyTo`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp` |
| No Javadoc on private methods | ✅ Only public methods documented |
| No documentation of internal/private fields | ✅ No field Javadoc added |
| `@param`, `@return`, `@throws` tags used | ✅ Throughout |
| `{@link}` cross-references used | ✅ Throughout |
| No implementation logic changed | ✅ Pure documentation additions |

| `buffer()` | Documents zero-copy duplicate semantics: independent position/limit but shared bytes; in-place mutation visibility. |
| `slice(int, int)` | Documents zero-copy sub-range view, silent clamping to available bytes, and all three `@throws IllegalArgumentException` conditions. |
| `batches()` | Documents the re-iterable behaviour, the lazy iterator, and the performance note that batch metadata is read without decompression. |
| `readableRecords(ByteBuffer)` | Documents no-copy semantics. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Documents all parameters. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long, int)` | Documents `maxSize` parameter; notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)` | Documents `logAppendTime` parameter. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)` | Notes auto-set log-append time. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, int)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch`. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, long, long, short, int, boolean)` | Documents transactional/idempotent overload (current magic, CREATE_TIME). |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int)` | Full producer-metadata overload without transactional flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, int)` | All fields except control-batch flag. |
| `builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, int)` | Full overload including `isControlBatch`. |
| `withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents convenience defaults. |
| `withRecords(Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `partitionLeaderEpoch` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic-version overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents `initialOffset` overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents magic + offset overload. |
| `withRecords(long, Compression, int, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents offset + leader-epoch overload. |
| `withIdempotentRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents idempotent producer identity fields and default values. |
| `withTransactionalRecords(...)` (all 3 overloads) | Documents transactional producer fields and default values. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, SimpleRecord...)` | Documents no-producer-metadata overload. |
| `withRecords(byte, long, Compression, TimestampType, long, short, int, int, boolean, SimpleRecord...)` | Full overload — documents all 10 parameters, the size-estimation strategy, single-batch guarantee, and `EMPTY` return for empty input. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents default offset=0 and current-time timestamp. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents explicit timestamp overload. |
| `withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, int, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)` | Documents full offset + epoch overload. |
| `writeEndTransactionalMarker(...)` | Documents as a lower-level buffer-writing alternative. |
| `withLeaderChangeMessage(...)` | Documents KRaft control-batch semantics (no compression, CREATE_TIME). |
| `withSnapshotHeaderRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withSnapshotFooterRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withKRaftVersionRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |
| `withVotersRecord(...)` | Same pattern. |

---

## Quality checklist

| Requirement | Status |
|-------------|--------|
| Thread-safety stated for every class | ✅ All three classes explicitly state "not thread-safe" |
| Performance implications noted for serialization/deserialization | ✅ `streamingIterator`, `validBytes`, `filterTo`, `sizeInBytes`, `writeFullyTo`, `offsetOfMaxTimestamp` |
| No Javadoc on private methods | ✅ Only public methods documented |
| No documentation of internal/private fields | ✅ No field Javadoc added |
| `@param`, `@return`, `@throws` tags used | ✅ Throughout |
| `{@link}` cross-references used | ✅ Throughout |
| No implementation logic changed | ✅ Pure documentation additions |
